spec_match.rationale: Uses getUserMedia directly and does not natively test or mount a real <usermedia> element.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive configurator with presets, video preview, negotiated settings panel, and live code output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Correctly presents how constraints should map to declarative <usermedia> attributes.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles errors during constraint negotiation gracefully, displaying details in the result card.

references.rationale: Contains correct references back to ChromeStatus.

design_system.rationale: Excellent use of system columns, monospace cards, contrast-safe coloring, and clear button layout.

openQuestions.title: Use <usermedia> element when supported
openQuestions.detail: When Chrome 150 is detected, the demo should instantiate the actual <usermedia> element with the configured attributes and data-constraints to let users test the native behavior instead of just calling standard getUserMedia().

summary: A fantastic interactive constraint configurator, but it uses getUserMedia directly and falls back to traditional scripting rather than exercising the new <usermedia> element.
